summary: A rectangular fortress-like building with four tall corner towers plus two additional tall towers along the long sides, raised on short stone footings with a flat dark red central roof and crenellated parapets. The main walls are light gray stone with narrow horizontal rows of windows; corner towers are darker gray stone with small slit windows and overhanging square platform tops framed in light slab material.

shape.overall_form: rectangular central hall surrounded by six tall towers (one at each corner and two on long sides), with raised base and parapet
shape.symmetry: bilaterally symmetrical along the long axis (mirrored left-right); roughly symmetrical front-back except tower spacing may differ

elements: multiple tall corner towers (4) and two mid-side towers (6 total)
raised single-block stone footings around perimeter
long rectangular central hall with three horizontal rows of narrow windows
flat dark-red (nether) roof with crenellations and a light slab border
tower platform caps with light slab frames and central glow (glowstone or lamp)
rebuild_hints: Place a rectangular main hall ~17x25 with walls of light stonebrick three stories tall and three horizontal bands of narrow 1x2 or 1x1 windows across each floor.

Add six tall square towers (approximately 3x3 or 4x4 columns) rising above hall at each corner and centered on long sides; towers use darker stonebrick, have small slit windows spaced vertically, and topped with an overhanging slab-framed platform with a central light.

Create a dark red (nether_brick) flat roof over the hall with a repeating crenellated edge and a light-colored slab border; add cobblestone or chunky footings along the base perimeter spaced every few blocks.
uncertainties: Exact block types: lighter wall may be stonebrick variants or andesite; tower darker blocks could be regular stonebrick or another gray variant.
Exact internal floor count and interior layout are not visible; floors_estimate inferred from window rows.
Precise footprint dimensions and tower thickness measured roughly from images; counts may vary by ±2 blocks.
Small decorative blocks (iron bars, slabs, glowstone) identified with moderate confidence but could be alternative blocks (fences, sea lanterns).
